title: Bool switches dont need values
body:
We want the persistent options for the podman command to not require a
true|false value when used.  That is to say, --syslog should set to
true when the default is false.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I don't think this is having the desired effect?
```
$ ./bin/podman --trace=false version
2019/05/01 13:46:24 Initializing logging reporter
Version:            1.3.0-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.11.7
Git Commit:         10d1cd2e9052b194049c5b3b012627ec67f25caf
Built:              Wed May  1 13:34:32 2019
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
2019/05/01 13:46:24 Reporting span 65a207b0b585df5d:65a207b0b585df5d:0:1
```
FWIW I don't think this is related to anything you did: it fails exactly the same way on master (as in, `--trace=false`, `--trace false`, `--trace=0`, all seem to enable tracing).
Probably just does an IsSet() instead of actually checking the value

I'm guessing you closed this because this approach didn't do what was expected? For benefit of future maintainers or anyone who might try this approach in the future, could you add a quick note here describing what the misunderstanding was? TIA.
